---
title: "cancer_marker"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "10/30/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(broom)
```

```{r}
species_data_800 = read_excel("data/species_866.xlsx")
ethnicity_800 = read_excel("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")
species_data_800
ethnicity_800
```

```{r}
## 1) ----- READ COEFFICIENT / INDEX TABLE -----
coef_df_raw <- read_xlsx("data/cancer_index.xlsx")
coef_df_raw
```

```{r}
## --------------------------
## Microbiome disease scores
## --------------------------

## PACKAGES
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)
library(broom)
library(tibble)

## --------------------------
## 0) Inputs you already have
## --------------------------
## species_data_800: data.frame with first column "FREG1_Barcode" then species columns (counts or RA)
## ethnicity_800:    data.frame with "FREG1_Barcode" and "FREG5_Ethnic_Group" (C/I/M)
## cancer index file: data/cancer_index.xlsx

## --------------------------
## 1) Read coefficient table
## --------------------------
coef_df_raw <- read_xlsx("data/cancer_index.xlsx")
names(coef_df_raw)[1] <- "feature"  # ensure first column is 'feature'

## make column names safe (e.g., IBS-D -> IBS_D)
names(coef_df_raw) <- gsub("-", "_", names(coef_df_raw))
coef_df_raw$feature <- gsub("_", " ", coef_df_raw$feature)

## choose diseases to score (exclude 'Health' from disease panel; we add it optionally later)
disease_cols <- setdiff(names(coef_df_raw), c("feature", "Health"))

coef_df <- coef_df_raw %>%
  select(feature, all_of(disease_cols)) %>%
  distinct(feature, .keep_all = TRUE)

## --------------------------
## 2) Align species between tables
## --------------------------
species_cols <- setdiff(colnames(species_data_800), "FREG1_Barcode")
common_species <- intersect(species_cols, coef_df$feature)

message(sprintf("Matched %d species (your table: %d, coef table: %d).",
                length(common_species), length(species_cols), nrow(coef_df)))

## --------------------------------------------
## 3) Normalize to relative abundance (row-wise)
## --------------------------------------------
abund_counts <- as.matrix(species_data_800[, common_species, drop = FALSE])
row_sums <- rowSums(abund_counts, na.rm = TRUE)
row_sums[row_sums == 0] <- 1  # avoid division by zero
abund_rel <- abund_counts / row_sums

## build sample-keyed matrix
abund_mat <- abund_rel
rownames(abund_mat) <- species_data_800$FREG1_Barcode

## --------------------------------------------
## 4) Build coefficient matrix (aligned in order)
## --------------------------------------------
coef_mat <- coef_df %>%
  filter(feature %in% common_species) %>%
  arrange(match(feature, common_species)) %>%
  column_to_rownames("feature") %>%
  as.matrix()

stopifnot(identical(colnames(abund_mat), rownames(coef_mat)))

## --------------------------------------------
## 5) Compute disease scores: A %*% beta
## --------------------------------------------
scores_mat <- abund_mat %*% coef_mat[, disease_cols, drop = FALSE]
scores_df <- scores_mat %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("FREG1_Barcode")

## optional: also compute a Health score for reference
if ("Health" %in% names(coef_df_raw)) {
  health_vec <- coef_df_raw %>%
    filter(feature %in% colnames(abund_mat)) %>%
    arrange(match(feature, colnames(abund_mat))) %>%
    pull(Health)
  scores_df$Health <- as.numeric(abund_mat %*% matrix(health_vec, ncol = 1))
}

## --------------------------------------------
## 6) Merge ethnicity (simple base merge)
## --------------------------------------------
eth_col <- "FREG5_Ethnic_Group"     # <--- change this if your column has a different name
meta_min <- ethnicity_800[, c("FREG1_Barcode", eth_col), drop = FALSE]

scores_annot <- merge(scores_df, meta_min, by = "FREG1_Barcode", all.x = TRUE)
names(scores_annot)[names(scores_annot) == eth_col] <- "Ethnicity"

## keep only C/I/M if present
scores_annot <- subset(scores_annot, Ethnicity %in% c("C","I","M"))

## quick sanity check
print(table(scores_annot$Ethnicity, useNA = "ifany"))


# Robust Kruskal–Wallis (skips if <2 groups)
safe_kruskal <- function(y, g) {
  g <- droplevels(factor(g))
  if (nlevels(g) < 2L) return(data.frame(statistic = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_))
  kt <- try(stats::kruskal.test(y ~ g), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(kt, "try-error")) return(data.frame(statistic = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_))
  data.frame(statistic = unname(kt$statistic), p_value = kt$p.value)
}

kw_res <- do.call(rbind, lapply(disease_cols, function(d) {
  y <- scores_annot[[d]]
  g <- scores_annot$Ethnicity
  out <- safe_kruskal(y, g)
  cbind(Disease = d, out, row.names = NULL)
}))

# Robust pairwise Wilcoxon (forces dimnames; skips empty)
safe_pairwise <- function(y, g) {
  g <- droplevels(factor(g))
  if (nlevels(g) < 2L) return(NULL)
  pw <- suppressWarnings(stats::pairwise.wilcox.test(y, g, p.adjust.method = "BH"))
  M <- pw$p.value
  if (is.null(M)) return(NULL)

  # Ensure row/col names exist (pairwise.wilcox.test can drop them)
  if (is.null(rownames(M)) || any(rownames(M) == "")) {
    # convention: rows = levels(g)[-1], cols = levels(g)[-n]
    rs <- levels(g)
    if (length(rs) >= 2L) {
      rownames(M) <- rs[-1]
      colnames(M) <- rs[-length(rs)]
    } else {
      return(NULL)
    }
  } else if (is.null(colnames(M)) || any(colnames(M) == "")) {
    cs <- levels(g)
    if (length(cs) >= 2L) {
      colnames(M) <- cs[-length(cs)]
    } else {
      return(NULL)
    }
  }

  # Long format without relying on as.table() keeping names
  out <- expand.grid(
    Group1 = rownames(M),
    Group2 = colnames(M),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  out$p_adj <- as.vector(M)
  out <- out[!is.na(out$p_adj), , drop = FALSE]
  if (!nrow(out)) return(NULL)
  out
}

pairwise_list <- do.call(rbind, lapply(disease_cols, function(d) {
  y <- scores_annot[[d]]
  g <- scores_annot$Ethnicity
  out <- safe_pairwise(y, g)
  if (is.null(out)) return(NULL)
  cbind(Disease = d, out, row.names = NULL)
}))

# (Optional) Save results
# if (!is.null(kw_res) && nrow(kw_res)) {
#   write.csv(kw_res, "figure/disease_scores_kw_ethnicity.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# }
# if (!is.null(pairwise_list) && nrow(pairwise_list)) {
#   write.csv(pairwise_list, "figure/disease_scores_pairwise_ethnicity.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# }


```
```{r}
pairwise_list
kw_res
scores_annot
```

```{r}
med_cols <- intersect(disease_cols, names(scores_annot))
mean_table <- aggregate(
  . ~ Ethnicity,
  data = scores_annot[, c("Ethnicity", med_cols), drop = FALSE],
  FUN  = function(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
)
print(mean_table)

```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)    # for stat_compare_means
library(patchwork) # for combining plots

## ---- CRC scores by ethnicity ----
crc_df <- scores_annot %>%
  select(Ethnicity, CRC) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Ethnicity), !is.na(CRC)) %>%
  mutate(Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity, levels = c("C","I","M")))

## ---- ASIR 2019 (per 100,000), read from Fig 6 (CRC panel) ----
asir_2019 <- tibble(
  Ethnicity  = factor(c("C","I","M"), levels = c("C","I","M")),
  ASIR_2019  = c(36.0, 19.0, 32.5)   # Chinese, Indian, Malay
)

## ---- APC for CRC age 20–49 by ethnicity (from Table 4) ----
apc_20_49 <- tibble(
  Ethnicity = factor(c("C","I","M"), levels = c("C","I","M")),
  APC_20_49 = c(0.23, 0.47, 2.90)    # CN: trend 1999–2019; IN: 1968–2019; MY: 1968–2019
)

## ---- summary table: mean CRC risk per ethnicity + ASIR + APC ----
summary_df <- crc_df %>%
  group_by(Ethnicity) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean_CRC = mean(CRC), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(asir_2019, by = "Ethnicity") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(apc_20_49, by = "Ethnicity")
summary_df

```
```{r}
## global & pairwise p-values (Kruskal–Wallis + Wilcoxon)
kruskal_res <- kruskal.test(CRC ~ Ethnicity, data = crc_df)
kruskal_res

pairwise_res <- pairwise.wilcox.test(crc_df$CRC, crc_df$Ethnicity,
                                     p.adjust.method = "BH")
pairwise_res
```


```{r}
scale_factor <- 0.2
comparisons_list <- list(c("C","I"), c("C","M"), c("I","M"))

p_violin <- ggplot(crc_df, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = CRC)) +
  ## ---- 1) ASIR line BELOW violins (drawn first) ----
  geom_line(data = asir_2019,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            aes(x = Ethnicity, y = ASIR_2019 * scale_factor, group = 1),
            color = "red", size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = asir_2019,
             inherit.aes = FALSE,
             aes(x = Ethnicity, y = ASIR_2019 * scale_factor),
             color = "red", size = 3) +
  geom_text(data = asir_2019,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            aes(x = Ethnicity, y = ASIR_2019 * scale_factor,
                label = round(ASIR_2019, 1)),
            vjust = -0.7, color = "red", size = 3.3) +

  ## ---- 2) Violins, boxplots, means ON TOP ----
  geom_violin(aes(fill = Ethnicity),
              trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15,
               outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("C"="#4E79A7","I"="#F28E2B","M"="#59A14F")) +

  ## ---- 3) Axes ----
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "CRC Microbial Risk Score",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / scale_factor,
                        name = "CRC ASIR 2019 (per 100,000)")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Ethnicity") +

  ## ---- 4) Pairwise p-values ONLY (no KW text) ----
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = comparisons_list,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.signif",
    step.increase = 0.1  # stacks the bars nicely
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "red"),
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(color = "red")
  )

p_violin
#ggsave("figure/violin_score_sue.pdf", p_violin, width = 12, height = 11, dpi = 300)

```
```{r}
## ============================
## 1. Packages
## ============================
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)

## ============================
## 2. Data prep
## ============================

# 2.1 CRC scores by ethnicity
crc_df <- scores_annot %>%
  select(Ethnicity, CRC) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Ethnicity), !is.na(CRC)) %>%
  # recode C/I/M to full labels
  mutate(
    Ethnicity = factor(
      Ethnicity,
      levels = c("C","I","M"),
      labels = c("Chinese","Indian","Malay")
    )
  )

# 2.2 ASIR 2019 from Figure 6 (CRC panel)
asir_2019 <- tibble(
  Ethnicity  = factor(c("Chinese","Indian","Malay"),
                      levels = c("Chinese","Indian","Malay")),
  ASIR_2019  = c(36.0, 19.0, 32.5)  # per 100,000 person-years
)

# 2.3 pairwise comparisons for p-values
comparisons_list <- list(
  c("Chinese","Indian"),
  c("Chinese","Malay"),
  c("Indian","Malay")
)
```


```{r}
## ============================
## 3. Plot
## ============================

scale_factor <- 0.10   # converts ASIR to score scale (36 -> 3.6)

p_violin <- ggplot(crc_df, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = CRC)) +
  # ---- ASIR line below ----
  geom_line(data = asir_2019,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            aes(Ethnicity, ASIR_2019 * scale_factor, group = 1),
            color = "#E9967A", size = 1) +
  geom_point(data = asir_2019,
             inherit.aes = FALSE,
             aes(Ethnicity, ASIR_2019 * scale_factor),
             color = "#E9967A", size = 2.8) +
  geom_text(data = asir_2019,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            aes(Ethnicity, ASIR_2019 * scale_factor, label = ASIR_2019),
            vjust = -0.8, color = "#E9967A", size = 3.2) +

  # ---- violins ----
  geom_violin(aes(fill = Ethnicity),
              trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7, colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.12,
               outlier.shape = NA,
               fill = "white", alpha = 0.9, colour = "black") +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "black", color = "black") +
#ethnicity_colors = c("C" = "#9FDDCA","I"= "#9EA3F1","M" = "#E17887","O" = "#80796BFF")
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Chinese"="#9FDDCA",
    "Indian" ="#9EA3F1",
    "Malay"  ="#E17887"
  )) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "CRC microbial risk score (standardised)",
    limits = c(-4, 5.2),
    breaks = seq(-4, 4, 2),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . / scale_factor,
      breaks = seq(0, 40, 10),
      name   = "CRC ASIR 2019 (per 100,000 person-years)"
    )
  ) +
  labs(x = "Ethnicity") +

  # ---- pairwise stats only ----
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = comparisons_list,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.signif",
    label.y = c(3.4, 3.9, 4.4)
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(color = "#E9967A"),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "#E9967A",
                                      margin = margin(l = 8)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 8)),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 8))
  )


p_violin

# ggsave("figure/CRC_risk_violin_sue.pdf",
#        p_violin, width = 7, height = 7)

```













```{r}
scores_annot$Ethnicity <- factor(scores_annot$Ethnicity, levels = c("C","I","M"))

# Long format for plotting
scores_long <- scores_annot %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(disease_cols), names_to = "Disease", values_to = "Score")

# Define pairwise comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("C", "I"), c("C", "M"), c("I", "M"))

# Faceted boxplots + jitter with matching colors and significance
p_all <- ggplot(scores_long, aes(Ethnicity, Score, fill = Ethnicity)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Ethnicity), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.35, size = 0.5) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, 
                     method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.signif",
                     size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ Disease, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Microbiome-derived disease scores by Ethnicity",
       x = NULL, y = "Score") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

print(p_all)

# save
#ggsave("figure/box_all_diseases_by_ethnicity.pdf", p_all, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)

# 1. Define custom colors
ethnicity_colors <- c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887")

# 2. Define the desired order of diseases
disease_order <- c("CRC", "CA", "CD", "UC", "IBS_D")

# 3. Filter data AND set the order
scores_long_filtered <- scores_long %>%
  filter(Disease %in% disease_order) %>%
  mutate(Disease = factor(Disease, levels = disease_order))

# 4. Plot
p_all <- ggplot(scores_long_filtered, aes(Ethnicity, Score, fill = Ethnicity)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  
  # tip.length = 0 removes the vertical downward ticks on the brackets
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, 
                     method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.signif",
                     size = 3,
                     tip.length = 0) + 
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ Disease, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) + 
  
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Microbiome-derived disease scores by Ethnicity",
       x = NULL, y = "Score") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# 5. Save with specific dimensions to fix the "squashed" look
# Adjust width and height as needed (e.g., width = 10, height = 4)
#ggsave("figure/box_all_diseases_by_ethnicity2.pdf", p_all, width = 12, height = 5)

print(p_all)
```





```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(corrplot)
library(ggplot2)

# ============================================================================
# PART 1: DATA PREPARATION
# ============================================================================

# Your mean disease risk scores from the uploaded table
# Lower score = less risk
# Ethnicity groups: C = Chinese, I = Indian, M = Malay

risk_scores <- data.frame(
  Ethnicity = c("C", "I", "M"),
  UC = c(-1.160720, -1.176304, -1.276898),
  CD = c(-8.232967, -9.602505, -9.331075),
  CA = c(0.09310305, -0.31742837, -0.14530807),
  CRC = c(-0.5067741, -1.1597219, -0.7438188),
  Obesity = c(0.03924769, -0.02590973, 0.13513383),
  CVD = c(0.4242326, -0.2404301, 0.1824113),
  IBS_D = c(-0.7374613, -1.0388377, -0.8515770)
)

# Singapore incidence/prevalence data (per 100,000 population or %)
# Sources: Multiple epidemiological studies from Singapore

singapore_incidence <- data.frame(
  Ethnicity = c("C", "I", "M"),
  UC  = c(6.0, 16.2, 7.0),        # per 100,000 (Ooi et al., Ann Acad Med SG 2000)
  CD  = c(3.6, 3.6, 3.6),         # same across (no ethnic diff)
  CRC = c(NA, NA, NA),            # no official stratified data
  CVD = c(58, 126, 114),          # per 100,000 (STEMI 2014)
  Obesity = c(7.9, 21.3, 26.2),   # % (NPHS 2022)
  CA  = c(NA, NA, NA),
  IBS_D = c(NA, NA, NA)
)

print("Disease Risk Scores:")
print(risk_scores)
print("\nSingapore Incidence/Prevalence Data:")
print(singapore_incidence)
```


```{r}
# ============================================================================
# PART 2: DATA RESHAPING FOR CORRELATION
# ============================================================================

# Reshape data to long format for analysis
risk_long <- risk_scores %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Ethnicity, 
               names_to = "Disease", 
               values_to = "RiskScore")

incidence_long <- singapore_incidence %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Ethnicity, 
               names_to = "Disease", 
               values_to = "Incidence")

# Combine risk scores and incidence data
combined_data <- risk_long %>%
  left_join(incidence_long, by = c("Ethnicity", "Disease")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Incidence))  # Remove diseases without incidence data

print("\nCombined Dataset (21 potential observations):")
print(combined_data)
print(paste("\nActual number of observations with complete data:", nrow(combined_data)))

# ============================================================================
# PART 3: CORRELATION ANALYSIS
# ============================================================================

# Overall correlation across all diseases and ethnicities
cat("\n" , rep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("CORRELATION ANALYSIS: Risk Score vs Actual Incidence\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n")

# Pearson correlation
pearson_cor <- cor.test(combined_data$RiskScore, 
                        combined_data$Incidence, 
                        method = "pearson")

# Spearman correlation (rank-based, more robust)
spearman_cor <- cor.test(combined_data$RiskScore, 
                         combined_data$Incidence, 
                         method = "spearman")

cat("Overall Correlation (all diseases combined):\n")
cat(sprintf("Pearson r = %.3f, p-value = %.4f\n", 
            pearson_cor$estimate, pearson_cor$p.value))
cat(sprintf("Spearman rho = %.3f, p-value = %.4f\n\n", 
            spearman_cor$estimate, spearman_cor$p.value))
```












```{r}
ethnicity_800
```

different crc risk scores
```{r}

species_data_800 = read_excel("data/species_866.xlsx") 
ethnicity_800 = read_excel("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")
```



```{r}
core_species_6 <- c(
  "Parvimonas micra",
  "Clostridium symbiosum",
  "Peptostreptococcus stomatis",
  "Bacteroides fragilis",
  "Gemella morbillorum",
  "Fusobacterium nucleatum"
)
species_27 <- c(
  "Bacteroides_fragilis",
  "Parvimonas_micra",
  "Clostridium_symbiosum",
  "Gemella_morbillorum",
  "Fusobacterium_nucleatum",
  "Anaerotignum_faecicola",
  "Bacteroides_caccae",
  "Bacteroides_nordii",
  "Hungatella_hathewayi",
  "Eisenbergiella_tayi",
  "Odoribacter_splanchnicus",
  "Intestinimonas_butyriciproducens",
  "Candidatus_Pararuminococcus_gallinarum",
  "GGB2998_SGB3988",
  "Anaerotruncus_colihominis",
  "GGB33512_SGB15201",
  "Peptostreptococcus_stomatis",
  "GGB9770_SGB15390",
  "Christensenella_minuta",
  "Intestinimonas_massiliensis",
  "Solobacterium_SGB6829",
  "Christensenella_hongkongensis",
  "Lawsonibacter_sp_NSJ_51",
  "Lachnospiraceae_bacterium_BX3",
  "Lachnospiraceae_bacterium_Marseille_Q4251",
  "GGB9758_SGB15368",
  "Streptococcus_salivarius"
)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(vegan)
library(tibble)

## helper to standardise names
clean_name <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
    str_squish() %>%
    str_to_lower()
}

## generic MRS calculator for any species set
compute_mrs_for_species <- function(species_vec,
                                    species_data,
                                    barcode_col = "FREG1_Barcode",
                                    prefix = "MRS") {
  # 1) build mapping between requested species and table columns
  col_df <- tibble(
    raw_colname   = names(species_data),
    clean_colname = clean_name(names(species_data))
  )
  
  target_df <- tibble(
    target_species    = species_vec,
    target_clean_name = clean_name(species_vec)
  )
  
  species_map <- target_df %>%
    left_join(col_df, by = c("target_clean_name" = "clean_colname"))
  
  message("=== Mapping for ", prefix, " ===")
  print(species_map)
  
  species_map_valid <- species_map %>%
    filter(!is.na(raw_colname))
  
  if (nrow(species_map_valid) < 2) {
    warning("Fewer than 2 species were matched for ", prefix,
            ". Scores will be based only on the matched species.")
  }
  
  # 2) extract matrix of those species
  mrs_data <- species_data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(barcode_col), all_of(species_map_valid$raw_colname))
  
  # replace NA with 0 in species columns
  mrs_data[is.na(mrs_data)] <- 0
  
  sub_mat <- as.matrix(mrs_data[, -1, drop = FALSE])
  
  # 3a) α-diversity MRS (Shannon of sub-community)
  row_sums <- rowSums(sub_mat)
  sub_rel  <- sweep(sub_mat, 1, row_sums, FUN = "/")
  sub_rel[is.na(sub_rel)] <- 0
  
  mrs_alpha <- diversity(sub_rel, index = "shannon")
  
  # 3b) sum-abundance MRS (like unweighted summation)
  mrs_sum <- rowSums(sub_mat)
  
  # 4) return a tibble with consistent column names
  out <- tibble(
    !!barcode_col := mrs_data[[barcode_col]],
    !!paste0(prefix, "_alpha") := mrs_alpha,
    !!paste0(prefix, "_sum")   := mrs_sum
  )
  
  return(out)
}

```

```{r}
## 1) 6-species scores (paper-recommended core MRS)
scores_6 <- compute_mrs_for_species(
  species_vec = core_species_6,
  species_data = species_data_800,
  barcode_col = "FREG1_Barcode",
  prefix = "CRC_MRS_6sp"
)

## 2) 27-species scores (full MMUPHin set)
scores_27 <- compute_mrs_for_species(
  species_vec = species_27,          # <-- your 27 names
  species_data = species_data_800,
  barcode_col = "FREG1_Barcode",
  prefix = "CRC_MRS_27sp"
)

## 3) Merge with ethnicity and store in crc_mrs_df
crc_mrs_df <- scores_6 %>%
  full_join(scores_27, by = "FREG1_Barcode") %>%
  left_join(
    ethnicity_800 %>%
      dplyr::select(FREG1_Barcode, FREG5_Ethnic_Group),
    by = "FREG1_Barcode"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Ethnicity = FREG5_Ethnic_Group
  )

head(crc_mrs_df)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# If Ethnicity is not a factor, convert it:
crc_mrs_df <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity, levels = c("C", "I", "M")))

# Boxplot with jittered points
ggplot(crc_mrs_df, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = CRC_MRS_27sp_alpha, fill = Ethnicity)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Ethnicity), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("C" = "#1f78b4", "I" = "#33a02c", "M" = "#e31a1c")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("C" = "#1f78b4", "I" = "#33a02c", "M" = "#e31a1c")) +
  labs(
    x = "Ethnic Group",
    y = "CRC Microbial Risk Score (MRSα)",
    title = "CRC Microbial Risk Score Across Ethnic Groups"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )
```

## 2 crc score


```{r}
```


```{r}
# 7 key genera from Tsai et al. 2025
tsai_genera <- c(
  "Porphyromonas",
  "Peptostreptococcus",
  "Parvimonas",
  "Fusobacterium",
  "Collinsella",
  "Haemophilus",
  "Atopobium"
)

# All species columns (assumes first column is FREG1_Barcode)
species_cols <- setdiff(names(species_data_800), "FREG1_Barcode")

# Map each species column to its genus (everything before first "_")
map_df <- tibble(
  col_name = species_cols,
  genus    = sub(" .*", "", species_cols)
)

# Keep only species that belong to the 7 target genera
tsai_species_cols <- map_df %>%
  filter(genus %in% tsai_genera) %>%
  pull(col_name)

tsai_species_cols   # check which columns were used

# Subset to barcode + these species
mrs_tsai <- species_data_800 %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, all_of(tsai_species_cols))

# Replace NAs in species with 0
mrs_tsai[is.na(mrs_tsai)] <- 0

# Matrix of abundances
sub_mat <- as.matrix(mrs_tsai[, -1, drop = FALSE])

# Convert to relative abundance within the 7-genera sub-community
row_sums <- rowSums(sub_mat)
sub_rel  <- sweep(sub_mat, 1, row_sums, FUN = "/")
sub_rel[is.na(sub_rel)] <- 0

# 1) Tsai_MRS_alpha7: Shannon diversity of these genera
Tsai_MRS_alpha7 <- diversity(sub_rel, index = "shannon")

# 2) Tsai_MRS_sum7: total abundance of these genera
Tsai_MRS_sum7 <- rowSums(sub_mat)

# Build a dataframe with the new MRS
tsai_mrs_df <- tibble(
  FREG1_Barcode = mrs_tsai$FREG1_Barcode,
  Tsai_MRS_alpha7 = Tsai_MRS_alpha7,
  Tsai_MRS_sum7   = Tsai_MRS_sum7
)
tsai_mrs_df
```

```{r}
crc_mrs_df <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  left_join(
    tsai_mrs_df %>%
      select(FREG1_Barcode, Tsai_MRS_alpha7, Tsai_MRS_sum7),
    by = "FREG1_Barcode"
  )

head(crc_mrs_df)


```

```{r}
ggplot(crc_mrs_df, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = Tsai_MRS_sum7, fill = Ethnicity)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Ethnicity), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("C" = "#1f78b4", "I" = "#33a02c", "M" = "#e31a1c")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("C" = "#1f78b4", "I" = "#33a02c", "M" = "#e31a1c")) +
  labs(
    x = "Ethnic Group",
    y = "CRC Microbial Risk Score (MRSα)",
    title = "CRC Microbial Risk Score Across Ethnic Groups"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )
```
# 3 crc score
```{r}
# 29 CRC-associated species from Thomas et al. (your screenshot)
species_29 <- c(
  "Parvimonas micra",
  "Gemella morbillorum",
  "Peptostreptococcus stomatis",
  "F. nucleatum subspecies animalis",
  "Unknown Dialister",
  "Unknown Porphyromonas",
  "Solobacterium moorei",
  "Porphyromonas uenonis",
  "Clostridium symbiosum",
  "Unknown Clostridiales",
  "Hungatella hathewayi",
  "Prevotella intermedia",
  "Porphyromonas somerae",
  "Porphyromonas asaccharolytica",
  "F. nucleatum subspecies nucleatum",
  "Parvimonas species",
  "Prevotella nigrescens",
  "Unknown Porphyromonas",
  "Ruminococcus torques",
  "F. nucleatum subspecies vincentii",
  "Fusobacterium species oral taxon 370",
  "Unknown Peptostreptococcaceae",
  "Anaerococcus obesiensis/vaginalis",
  "Unknown Anaerotruncus",
  "P. uenonis",
  "Unknown Clostridiales",
  "Unknown Porphyromonas",
  "Clostridium bolteae/clostridioforme",
  "Subdoligranulum species"
)

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(vegan)
library(tibble)

# 1) Which of the 29 species actually appear as columns in your table?
species_cols_present <- intersect(species_29, names(species_data_800))

length(species_cols_present)
species_cols_present   # check this in the console

if (length(species_cols_present) < 2) {
  warning("Fewer than 2 of the 29 CRC species were found in species_data_800. ",
          "The 29-species MRS will be based only on the matched species.")
}

# 2) Subset to barcode + matched species
mrs29_data <- species_data_800 %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, all_of(species_cols_present))

# Replace NAs in these species with 0
mrs29_data[is.na(mrs29_data)] <- 0

# 3) Abundance matrix
sub_mat_29 <- as.matrix(mrs29_data[, -1, drop = FALSE])

# 4a) CRC_MRS_29sp_alpha: Shannon diversity of this sub-community
row_sums_29 <- rowSums(sub_mat_29)
sub_rel_29  <- sweep(sub_mat_29, 1, row_sums_29, FUN = "/")
sub_rel_29[is.na(sub_rel_29)] <- 0

CRC_MRS_29sp_alpha <- diversity(sub_rel_29, index = "shannon")

# 4b) CRC_MRS_29sp_sum: total abundance of the matched 29 species
CRC_MRS_29sp_sum <- rowSums(sub_mat_29)

# 5) Put scores in a small df
mrs29_df <- tibble(
  FREG1_Barcode      = mrs29_data$FREG1_Barcode,
  CRC_MRS_29sp_alpha = CRC_MRS_29sp_alpha,
  CRC_MRS_29sp_sum   = CRC_MRS_29sp_sum
)

```
```{r}
crc_mrs_df <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  left_join(mrs29_df, by = "FREG1_Barcode")

head(crc_mrs_df)

```
```{r}
ggplot(crc_mrs_df, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, fill = Ethnicity)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Ethnicity), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("C" = "#1f78b4", "I" = "#33a02c", "M" = "#e31a1c")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("C" = "#1f78b4", "I" = "#33a02c", "M" = "#e31a1c")) +
  labs(
    x = "Ethnic Group",
    y = "CRC Microbial Risk Score (MRSα)",
    title = "CRC Microbial Risk Score Across Ethnic Groups"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# 1) Identify all score columns automatically (everything except barcode + ethnicity)
score_cols <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  select(-FREG1_Barcode, -Ethnicity) %>%
  names()

score_cols
crc_mrs_means <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Ethnicity) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(all_of(score_cols), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

crc_mrs_means


```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

# 1️⃣ Identify score columns (everything except ID + Ethnicity)
score_cols <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  dplyr::select(-FREG1_Barcode, -Ethnicity) %>%
  names()

# 2️⃣ Long format for ggplot
crc_mrs_long <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(score_cols),
    names_to = "Score",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity, levels = c("C", "I", "M")),
    # Optional: order scores in a nicer way
    Score = fct_relevel(
      Score,
      "CRC_MRS_6sp_alpha", "CRC_MRS_6sp_sum",
      "CRC_MRS_27sp_alpha", "CRC_MRS_27sp_sum",
      "CRC_MRS_29sp_alpha", "CRC_MRS_29sp_sum",
      "Tsai_MRS_alpha7", "Tsai_MRS_sum7"
      # add other names here if you have more
    )
  )

# 3️⃣ Faceted boxplots
ggplot(crc_mrs_long, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = Value, fill = Ethnicity)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.4, size = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Score, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Ethnicity",
    y = "Score value",
    title = "CRC-related microbial scores across ethnic groups"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

## 1️⃣ Identify score columns (everything except ID + Ethnicity)
score_cols <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  dplyr::select(-FREG1_Barcode, -Ethnicity) %>%
  names()

## 2️⃣ Compute RAW MEANS by ethnicity (drop NA ethnicity)
crc_mrs_means <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Ethnicity)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Ethnicity) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(all_of(score_cols), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

## 3️⃣ Long format + Z-SCORE (for color only)
crc_mrs_means_long <- crc_mrs_means %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(score_cols),
    names_to = "Score",
    values_to = "Mean"
  )

crc_mrs_means_z <- crc_mrs_means_long %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Score) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Zscore = (Mean - mean(Mean, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(Mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity, levels = c("C", "I", "M")),
    # optional: order scores nicely if you like
    Score = fct_relevel(
      Score,
      "CRC_MRS_6sp_alpha", "CRC_MRS_6sp_sum",
      "CRC_MRS_27sp_alpha", "CRC_MRS_27sp_sum",
      "CRC_MRS_29sp_alpha", "CRC_MRS_29sp_sum",
      "Tsai_MRS_alpha7", "Tsai_MRS_sum7"
    )
  )

## 4️⃣ Heatmap: color = standardized Zscore, text = RAW mean
ggplot(crc_mrs_means_z, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = Score, fill = Zscore)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  # raw mean printed in each box
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", Mean)), size = 3) +
  # diverging color scale for standardized values
  scale_fill_distiller(
    type = "div",
    palette = "RdBu",
    direction = 1,
    name = "Z-score"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "CRC microbial risk scores by ethnicity",
    x = "Ethnicity",
    y = "Score"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )


```
## VIOLIN  for CRC_MRS_29sp_sum
```{r}
crc_mrs_df
scores_annot
merged_df <- merge(
  scores_annot[, c("FREG1_Barcode", "UC", "CD", "CA", "CRC", "IBS_D", "Ethnicity")],  # select columns you want
  crc_mrs_df[, c("FREG1_Barcode", "CRC_MRS_29sp_sum")],       # select columns you want
  by = "FREG1_Barcode",
  all.x = TRUE
)

# Remove FREG1_Barcode and rename CRC_MRS_29sp_sum to CRC_Nature_Med
merged_df <- merged_df[, !names(merged_df) %in% "FREG1_Barcode"]

# Find and rename the CRC_MRS column
crc_col <- grep("CRC_MRS", names(merged_df))
if(length(crc_col) > 0) {
  names(merged_df)[crc_col] <- "CRC_Nature_Med"
}
merged_df
# Save
library(writexl)
write_xlsx(merged_df, "data/Sup3_risk_scores.xlsx")
```
```{r}

# 2.1 Join CRC_MRS_29sp_sum with ethnicity
crc29_df <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, CRC_MRS_29sp_sum) %>%
  inner_join(
    ethnicity_800 %>%
      select(FREG1_Barcode, FREG5_Ethnic_Group),
    by = "FREG1_Barcode"
  ) %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M")) %>%
  mutate(
    Ethnicity = recode(FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
                       C = "Chinese",
                       I = "Indian",
                       M = "Malay"),
    Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity, levels = c("Chinese", "Indian", "Malay"))
  )

# 2.1b Remove outliers per ethnic group
crc29_df_filtered <- crc29_df %>%
  group_by(Ethnicity) %>%
  mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    lower_bound = Q1 - 1.2 * IQR,
    upper_bound = Q3 + 1.2 * IQR
  ) %>%
  filter(CRC_MRS_29sp_sum >= lower_bound & CRC_MRS_29sp_sum <= upper_bound) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, FREG5_Ethnic_Group, Ethnicity)

# 2.2 ASIR 2019 for CRC (per 100,000 person-years)
asir_2019 <- tibble(
  Ethnicity  = factor(c("Chinese","Indian","Malay"),
                      levels = c("Chinese","Indian","Malay")),
  ASIR_2019  = c(36.0, 19.0, 32.5)
)

## ============================
## 3. Plot settings
## ============================
# Calculate y-axis limits based on filtered data
y_min <- min(crc29_df_filtered$CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, na.rm = TRUE)
y_max <- max(crc29_df_filtered$CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, na.rm = TRUE)

# Add some padding for the ASIR line
y_max <- y_max * 1.2

# Scale factor for ASIR
max_asir <- max(asir_2019$ASIR_2019)
scale_factor <- y_max / max_asir * 0.8

# New color scheme
eth_cols2 <- c(
  "Chinese" = "#1F6F5D",
  "Indian"  = "#2C2F88",
  "Malay"   = "#7E2236"
)

## ============================
## 4. Violin + ASIR plot for CRC_MRS_29sp_sum
## ============================
p_violin_29 <- ggplot(crc29_df_filtered, aes(x = Ethnicity, y = CRC_MRS_29sp_sum)) +
  # Violins
  geom_violin(aes(fill = Ethnicity),
              trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7, colour = NA) +
  
  # Boxplots without outliers
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.12,
               outlier.shape = NA,
               fill = "white", alpha = 0.9, colour = "black") +
  
  # Mean points
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, shape = 21,
               fill = "black", colour = "black") +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = eth_cols2) +
  
  # ASIR line
  geom_line(data = asir_2019,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            aes(x = Ethnicity,
                y = ASIR_2019 * scale_factor,
                group = 1),
            color = "#E9967A", size = 1) +
  geom_point(data = asir_2019,
             inherit.aes = FALSE,
             aes(x = Ethnicity,
                 y = ASIR_2019 * scale_factor),
             color = "#E9967A", size = 2.8) +
  geom_text(data = asir_2019,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            aes(x = Ethnicity,
                y = ASIR_2019 * scale_factor,
                label = ASIR_2019),
            vjust = -0.8, color = "#E9967A", size = 3.2) +
  
  # Axes
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "CRC 29-species microbial risk score",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . / scale_factor,
      breaks = seq(0, 40, 10),
      name   = "CRC ASIR 2019 (per 100,000 person-years)"
    )
  ) +
  labs(x = "Ethnicity") +
  
  # Theme
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(color = "#E9967A"),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "#E9967A",
                                      margin = margin(l = 8)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 8)),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 8))
  )

p_violin_29
```


```{r}
## 5. Save
# ggsave("CRC_MRS_29sp_violin_ASIR_std.png",
#        p_violin_29, width = 7, height = 4.5, dpi = 600)
ggsave("figure/CRC_29sp_violin.pdf",
       p_violin_29, width = 7, height = 6)

```





## heatmap for sue
```{r}
library(tidyverse)

## ---- 1. CRC coefficients ----
coef_crc <- coef_df_raw %>%
  select(feature, CRC) %>%
  filter(CRC != 0)       # only species used in CRC score

# all species available in your abundance table
all_species <- colnames(species_data_800)

# species that exist in BOTH coef table and abundance table
matched_species   <- intersect(coef_crc$feature, all_species)
unmatched_species <- setdiff(coef_crc$feature, matched_species)

length(matched_species)    # how many you can actually use
length(unmatched_species)  # how many are missing

# if you want to see which are missing:
unmatched_species

# keep only matched species + sign
coef_crc_matched <- coef_crc %>%
  filter(feature %in% matched_species) %>%
  mutate(sign = if_else(CRC > 0, "Positive", "Negative"))

crc_species <- coef_crc_matched$feature   # this is now SAFE for all_of()


```

```{r}
species_crc <- species_data_800 %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, all_of(crc_species))

eth_df <- ethnicity_800 %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C","I","M")) %>%
  mutate(
    Ethnicity = recode(FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
                       C = "Chinese", I = "Indian", M = "Malay"),
    Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity, levels = c("Chinese","Indian","Malay"))
  )

merged_crc <- species_crc %>%
  inner_join(eth_df, by = "FREG1_Barcode")




```

```{r}
library(forcats)

mean_by_eth <- merged_crc %>%
  group_by(Ethnicity) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(crc_species), ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

heat_long <- mean_by_eth %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(crc_species),
    names_to  = "feature",
    values_to = "mean_abundance"
  ) %>%
  left_join(coef_crc_matched, by = "feature") %>%  # adds CRC + sign
  group_by(feature) %>%
  mutate(z_abundance = as.numeric(scale(mean_abundance))) %>%
  ungroup()

# quick check: MUST see 'sign' here
str(heat_long)


```
```{r}
gg_heat_crc <- ggplot(
  heat_long,
  aes(x = Ethnicity,
      y = fct_reorder(feature, CRC),
      fill = z_abundance)
) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  facet_grid(sign ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#7FB3C8",     # muted blue
    mid = "#F8F8F8",
    high = "#EFA08A",    # softer coral
    midpoint = 0,
    name = "Z-score"
    ) +
  labs(
    x = "Ethnicity",
    y = "CRC signature species",
    title = "Mean abundance of CRC score species by ethnicity",
    subtitle = "Positive vs negative CRC coefficients"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )
gg_heat_crc <- gg_heat_crc +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(t = 20, r = 25, b = 10, l = 3),  # more space around plot
    plot.title = element_text(
      margin = margin(b = 6), 
      hjust = 0.5              # optional: center title
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      margin = margin(b = 10)
    )
  )
gg_heat_crc
ggsave("figure/CRC_sp_mean_abundance_heatmap_sue.png", gg_heat_crc, width = 5, height = 8, dpi = 300)

```
```{r}
coef_crc_matched
```
```{r}
crc_mrs_df
```
## qqplot
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Merge the datasets and filter for C, I, M only
plot_data <- crc_mrs_df %>%
  left_join(ethnicity_800, by = "FREG1_Barcode") %>%
  # Filter for only Chinese, Indian, Malay
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M")) %>%
  # Remove any missing values
  filter(!is.na(CRC_MRS_29sp_sum)) %>%
  # Rank the scores
  arrange(CRC_MRS_29sp_sum) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number())

# Create the dot plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = rank, y = CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, color = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73"),
                     name = "Ethnic Group",
                     labels = c("C" = "Chinese", "I" = "Indian", "M" = "Malay")) +
  labs(x = "Ranked Sample",
       y = "CRC Microbial Risk Score",
       title = "Distribution of CRC Risk Scores by Ethnicity") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)

# 1️⃣ Select only the two columns you care about
df_med <- plot_data %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, CRC_MRS_29sp_sum, FREG5_Ethnic_Group)

df_com <- scores_annot %>%
  select(FREG1_Barcode, CRC)

# 2️⃣ Merge these two small data frames
merged_scores <- df_med %>%
  inner_join(df_com, by = "FREG1_Barcode")
merged_scores
# 3️⃣ Rename the columns in the merged data frame
merged_scores <- merged_scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    crc_nature_med = CRC_MRS_29sp_sum,
    crc_nature_com = CRC
  )

# 4️⃣ Save to Excel
write_xlsx(merged_scores, "data/merged_crc_scores.xlsx")
merged_scores

```



```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggridges)

eth_cols2 <- c(
  "C" = "#1F6F5D",
  "I" = "#2C2F88",
  "M" = "#7E2236"
)

ethnicity_colors <- c(
  "C" = "#9FDDCA",
  "I" = "#9EA3F1",
  "M" = "#E17887"
)

# 1️⃣ Make long data and compute global ranks per score type
merged_long_rank <- merged_scores %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M")) %>%
  mutate(
    FREG5_Ethnic_Group = factor(FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
                                levels = c("C", "M", "I"))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(crc_nature_med, crc_nature_com),
    names_to  = "ScoreType",
    values_to = "Score"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ScoreType = recode(
      ScoreType,
      crc_nature_med = "Nature Med 29sp",
      crc_nature_com = "Nat Comms 50sp"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(ScoreType) %>%
  mutate(
    Rank = rank(Score, ties.method = "first")
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Add a column to assign colors based on ScoreType
  mutate(
    fill_color = case_when(
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C" ~ eth_cols2["C"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I" ~ eth_cols2["I"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M" ~ eth_cols2["M"],
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C" ~ ethnicity_colors["C"],
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I" ~ ethnicity_colors["I"],
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M" ~ ethnicity_colors["M"]
    )
  )

# 2️⃣ Ridgeline plot with different colors per facet
density_crc = ggplot(
  merged_long_rank,
  aes(x = Rank,
      y = FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
      fill = fill_color,
      group = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)
) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha = 0.7,
    color = "black",
    scale = 0.9,
    rel_min_height = 0.001
  ) +
  scale_fill_identity() +  # Use the actual color values
  facet_wrap(~ ScoreType, ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of CRC risk score ranks by ethnicity",
    subtitle = "Ethnic groups overlap within each microbiome-derived CRC score",
    x = "Rank position",
    y = "Ethnic group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()
  )

density_crc
ggsave("figure/density_crc.pdf", density_crc, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggridges)

ethnicity_colors <- c(
  "C" = "#9FDDCA",
  "I" = "#9EA3F1",
  "M" = "#E17887"
)

# 1️⃣ Long format + global ranking for each score
merged_long_rank <- merged_scores %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M")) %>%
  mutate(
    FREG5_Ethnic_Group = factor(FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
                                levels = c("C", "M", "I"))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(crc_nature_med, crc_nature_com),
    names_to  = "ScoreType",
    values_to = "Score"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ScoreType = recode(
      ScoreType,
      crc_nature_med = "Nature Med 29sp",
      crc_nature_com = "Nat Comms 50sp"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(ScoreType) %>%
  mutate(
    Rank = rank(Score, ties.method = "first")   # global rank
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2️⃣ Overlapping ridgelines (transparent)
density_crc_overlap = ggplot(
  merged_long_rank,
  aes(x = Rank,
      y = ScoreType,                    # all ethnic groups overlap on each panel
      fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group
  )
) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha = 0.6,                       # transparency
    color = "black",
    scale = 1.2,                        # slight inflation so curves overlap nicely
    rel_min_height = 0.001,
    position = "identity"               # THIS allows overlap
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of CRC risk score ranks by ethnicity",
    subtitle = "Ethnic groups overlap within each microbiome-derived CRC score",
    x = "Rank position",
    y = "CRC microbial risk score model",
    fill = "Ethnic group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()
  )
density_crc_overlap
ggsave("figure/density_crc_overlap.pdf", density_crc_overlap, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```
# correlation
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

asir_2019 <- tibble(
  Ethnicity  = factor(c("Chinese","Indian","Malay"),
                      levels = c("Chinese","Indian","Malay")),
  ASIR_2019  = c(36.0, 19.0, 32.5)  # per 100,000 person-years
)

ethnicity_colors_named <- c(
  "Chinese" = "#9FDDCA",  # C
  "Indian"  = "#9EA3F1",  # I
  "Malay"   = "#E17887"   # M
)

```

```{r}
score_summary <- merged_scores %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C","I","M")) %>%
  mutate(
    Ethnicity = recode(FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
                       "C" = "Chinese",
                       "I" = "Indian",
                       "M" = "Malay")
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(crc_nature_com, crc_nature_med),
    names_to  = "ScoreType",
    values_to = "Score"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ScoreType = recode(
      ScoreType,
      crc_nature_com = "Nat Comms 50sp",
      crc_nature_med = "Nature Med 29sp"
    ),
    Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity,
                       levels = c("Chinese","Indian","Malay"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(ScoreType, Ethnicity) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_score = mean(Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1           = quantile(Score, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3           = quantile(Score, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    n            = n(),
    .groups      = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(asir_2019, by = "Ethnicity")
score_summary
```
```{r}
cor_stats <- score_summary %>%
  group_by(ScoreType) %>%
  summarise(
    r = cor(mean_score, ASIR_2019, method = "pearson"),
    #p = cor.test(mean_score, ASIR_2019)$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    label = sprintf("Pearson r = %.3f", r)
  )

```
```{r}
library(ggh4x)

# Define both color schemes
ethnicity_colors_named <- c(
  "Chinese" = "#9FDDCA",
  "Indian"  = "#9EA3F1",
  "Malay"   = "#E17887"
)

eth_cols2 <- c(
  "Chinese" = "#1F6F5D",
  "Indian"  = "#2C2F88",
  "Malay"   = "#7E2236"
)


# Add a column to assign colors based on ScoreType
score_summary <- score_summary %>%
  mutate(
    fill_color = case_when(
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & Ethnicity == "Chinese" ~ ethnicity_colors_named["Chinese"],
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & Ethnicity == "Indian" ~ ethnicity_colors_named["Indian"],
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & Ethnicity == "Malay" ~ ethnicity_colors_named["Malay"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & Ethnicity == "Chinese" ~ eth_cols2["Chinese"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & Ethnicity == "Indian" ~ eth_cols2["Indian"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & Ethnicity == "Malay" ~ eth_cols2["Malay"]
    )
  )

label_positions <- score_summary %>%
  mutate(
    label_x = case_when(
      Ethnicity == "Chinese" ~ mean_score,
      Ethnicity == "Indian"  ~ mean_score,
      Ethnicity == "Malay"   ~ mean_score
    ),
    label_y = case_when(
      Ethnicity == "Chinese" ~ ASIR_2019 + 15,
      Ethnicity == "Indian"  ~ ASIR_2019 - 10,
      Ethnicity == "Malay"   ~ ASIR_2019 + 10
    )
  )

corre_cancer = ggplot(score_summary,
       aes(x = mean_score,
           y = ASIR_2019)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = TRUE,
              color = "darkred",
              linetype = "dashed",
              fill = "grey80",
              size = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n,
                 fill = fill_color),
             shape = 21,
             color = "black",
             alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text(
    data = label_positions,
    aes(x = label_x, y = label_y, label = Ethnicity),
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-150, 200)) +
  facet_wrap(~ ScoreType, scales = "free_x") +
  facetted_pos_scales(
    x = list(
      scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1.2, -0.3)),    # For Nat Comms (first facet)
      scale_x_continuous(limits = c(7300, 9000))     # For Nature Med (second facet)
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_identity() +  # Use the actual color values
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Sample size",
                        range = c(4, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation: CRC incidence vs microbiome-derived CRC scores",
    x     = "Mean CRC microbial risk score",
    y     = "CRC incidence (ASIR 2019, per 100,000 person-years)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = cor_stats,
    aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = label),
    hjust = -0.05, vjust = 1.3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.5
  )

corre_cancer
ggsave("figure/corre_cancer_score.pdf", corre_cancer, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Define both color schemes
ethnicity_colors_named <- c(
  "Chinese" = "#9FDDCA",
  "Indian"  = "#9EA3F1",
  "Malay"   = "#E17887"
)

eth_cols2 <- c(
  "Chinese" = "#1F6F5D",
  "Indian"  = "#2C2F88",
  "Malay"   = "#7E2236"
)

# Separate the two score types
nat_comms <- score_summary %>% filter(ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp")
nature_med <- score_summary %>% filter(ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp")

# Scale Nature Med scores to fit on same axis as Nat Comms
# We'll transform: new_x = (x - min) / (max - min) * (target_max - target_min) + target_min
nat_comms_range <- c(-1.2, -0.3)
nature_med_range <- c(7300, 8950)

nature_med <- nature_med %>%
  mutate(
    mean_score_scaled = (mean_score - nature_med_range[1]) / 
                        (nature_med_range[2] - nature_med_range[1]) * 
                        (nat_comms_range[2] - nat_comms_range[1]) + 
                        nat_comms_range[1]
  )

# Combine data
plot_data <- bind_rows(
  nat_comms %>% mutate(mean_score_plot = mean_score),
  nature_med %>% mutate(mean_score_plot = mean_score_scaled)
) %>%
  mutate(
    fill_color = case_when(
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & Ethnicity == "Chinese" ~ ethnicity_colors_named["Chinese"],
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & Ethnicity == "Indian" ~ ethnicity_colors_named["Indian"],
      ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp" & Ethnicity == "Malay" ~ ethnicity_colors_named["Malay"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & Ethnicity == "Chinese" ~ eth_cols2["Chinese"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & Ethnicity == "Indian" ~ eth_cols2["Indian"],
      ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp" & Ethnicity == "Malay" ~ eth_cols2["Malay"]
    )
  )

# Calculate correlations for both scores
cor_nat_comms <- cor.test(nat_comms$mean_score, nat_comms$ASIR_2019)
cor_nature_med <- cor.test(nature_med$mean_score, nature_med$ASIR_2019)

# Create transformation function for secondary axis
transform_to_nature_med <- function(x) {
  (x - nat_comms_range[1]) / (nat_comms_range[2] - nat_comms_range[1]) * 
    (nature_med_range[2] - nature_med_range[1]) + nature_med_range[1]
}

corre_cancer = ggplot(plot_data,
       aes(x = mean_score_plot,
           y = ASIR_2019)) +
  geom_smooth(data = plot_data %>% filter(ScoreType == "Nat Comms 50sp"),
              method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#9FDDCA", 
              linetype = "dashed", fill = "grey80", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(data = plot_data %>% filter(ScoreType == "Nature Med 29sp"),
              method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#2C2F88",
              linetype = "dashed", fill = "grey80", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n, fill = fill_color, shape = ScoreType),
             color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Ethnicity, color = ScoreType),
            hjust = -0.15, vjust = 0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold",
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Nat Comms 50sp score",
    limits = nat_comms_range,
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      trans = ~ transform_to_nature_med(.),
      name = "Nature Med 29sp score"
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Nat Comms 50sp" = "#9FDDCA", 
                                  "Nature Med 29sp" = "#2C2F88"),
                     guide = "none") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Nat Comms 50sp" = 21, "Nature Med 29sp" = 22),
                     name = "Score Type") +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Sample size", range = c(4, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation: CRC incidence vs microbiome-derived CRC scores",
    y = "CRC incidence (ASIR 2019, per 100,000 person-years)"
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = -1.15, y = 95, 
           label = sprintf("Nat Comms: r = %.3f", cor_nat_comms$estimate),
           hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = "#1F6F5D") +
  annotate("text", x = -1.15, y = 85,
           label = sprintf("Nature Med: r = %.3f", cor_nature_med$estimate),
           hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = "#2C2F88") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x.top = element_text(color = "#2C2F88"),
    axis.text.x.top = element_text(color = "#2C2F88"),
    axis.title.x.bottom = element_text(color = "#1F6F5D"),
    axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(color = "#1F6F5D")
  )

corre_cancer

#ggsave("figure/corre_cancer_score.pdf", corre_cancer, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 600)
```
